\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } k \in \mathbf{N}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \alpha, \beta \in \mathbf{R}.\\
&\text{Definition: } a = (a_1, \dots, a_k) \in \mathbf{R}^k.\\
&\text{Definition: } p(t) = a_1 + a_2 t + \cdots + a_k t^{k-1}, \quad t \in \mathbf{R}.\\
&\text{Definition: } S = \{a \in \mathbf{R}^k \mid p(0) = 1,\ |p(t)| \leq 1 \text{ for all } t \in [\alpha, \beta]\}.\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } \alpha \leq \beta.\\
&\text{Goal: determine whether } S \text{ is convex.}
\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } C \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n.\\
&\text{Definition: } \alpha, \beta \text{ are scalars.}\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } \alpha \ge 0,\ \beta \ge 0.\\
&\text{Definition: } \alpha C = \{\alpha x \mid x \in C\}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \alpha C + \beta C = \{u+v \mid u \in \alpha C,\ v \in \beta C\}.\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } C \text{ is convex if and only if } (\alpha + \beta)C = \alpha C + \beta C \\
&\text{for all nonnegative scalars } \alpha, \beta.
\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } A \in \mathbf{R}^{m\times n},\ b \in \mathbf{R}^m,\ c \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ d \in \mathbf{R},\ E \in \mathbf{R}^{p\times m},\ f \in \mathbf{R}^p,\ g \in \mathbf{R}^m,\ h \in \mathbf{R}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \phi : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}^m \text{ is defined by } \phi(x) = \frac{Ax+b}{c^T x + d}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \psi : \mathbf{R}^m \to \mathbf{R}^p \text{ is defined by } \psi(y) = \frac{Ey+f}{g^T y + h}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \mathbf{dom}\,\phi = \{x \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid c^T x + d > 0\}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \mathbf{dom}\,\psi = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^m \mid g^T y + h > 0\}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \phi \text{ is associated with the matrix } \begin{bmatrix} A & b \\ c^T & d \end{bmatrix}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \psi \text{ is associated with the matrix } \begin{bmatrix} E & f \\ g^T & h \end{bmatrix}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \Gamma = \psi \circ \phi.\\
&\text{Definition: } \Gamma(x) = \psi(\phi(x)).\\
&\text{Definition: } \mathbf{dom}\,\Gamma = \{x \in \mathbf{dom}\,\phi \mid \phi(x) \in \mathbf{dom}\,\psi\}.\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } \Gamma \text{ is linear-fractional.}\\
&\text{Goal: prove that the associated matrix of } \Gamma \text{ is } \begin{bmatrix} E & f \\ g^T & h \end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix} A & b \\ c^T & d \end{bmatrix}. 
\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } K_{\exp} \subseteq \mathbf{R}^3.\\
&\text{Definition: } K_{\exp} = \{(x,y,z) \in \mathbf{R}^3 \mid y > 0,\ ye^{x/y} \leq z\}.\\
&\text{Definition: } K_{\exp}^* = \{u \in \mathbf{R}^3 \mid u^T v \geq 0 \text{ for all } v \in K_{\exp}\}.\\

&\text{Hypothesis: Ignore boundary subtleties.}\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } K_{\exp} \text{ is not closed.}\\
&\text{Hypothesis: A cone and its closure have the same dual cone.}\\
&\text{Goal: determine } K_{\exp}^*.
\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } C, D \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n.\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } C \text{ and } D \text{ are closed convex cones.}\\
&\text{Definition: } \text{For a cone } K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n,\ K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}.\\
&\text{Definition: } C^* + D^* = \{u + v \mid u \in C^*,\ v \in D^*\}.\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } C \cap D \text{ and } C^* + D^* \text{ are convex cones.}
\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } C, D \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n \text{ are cones.}\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } C \text{ and } D \text{ are closed and convex.}\\
&\text{Definition: } \text{For a cone } K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n,\ K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}.\\
&\text{Definition: } C^* + D^* = \{u+v \mid u \in C^*,\ v \in D^*\}.\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } (C \cap D)^* \supseteq C^* + D^*.
\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } C \text{ and } D \text{ are closed convex cones in } \mathbf{R}^n.\\
&\text{Definition: } K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n \text{ is a cone.}\\
&\text{Definition: } K^* = \{y \in \mathbf{R}^n \mid y^T x \ge 0 \text{ for all } x \in K\}.\\
&\text{Definition: } C^* + D^* = \{u+v \mid u \in C^*,\ v \in D^*\}.\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } \text{if } K \subseteq \mathbf{R}^n \text{ is a closed convex cone, then } K^{**} = K.\\
&\text{Goal: show that } (C \cap D)^* \subseteq C^* + D^*.\\
&\text{Goal: first show that } (C \cap D)^* \subseteq C^* + D^* \Longleftrightarrow C \cap D \supseteq (C^* + D^*)^*.\\
&\text{Goal: then prove that } C \cap D \supseteq (C^* + D^*)^*.\\
&\text{Goal: conclude that } (C \cap D)^* = C^* + D^*.
\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{aligned}

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } f:\mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is convex.}\\
&\text{Definition: } h:\mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R} \text{ is called monotone nondecreasing if } h(x) \ge h(y) \text{ whenever } x \ge y \text{ componentwise.}\\
&\text{Definition: } g(x) = \inf_{z \in \mathbf{R}^n,\ z \ge 0} f(x+z), \quad x \in \mathbf{R}^n.\\

&\text{Hypothesis: } g(x) > -\infty \text{ for all } x \in \mathbf{R}^n.\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } g \text{ is convex.}\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } g \text{ is monotone nondecreasing.}\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } g(x) \le f(x) \text{ for all } x \in \mathbf{R}^n.\\
&\text{Definition: } h:\mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R} \text{ is any convex function that is monotone nondecreasing and satisfies } h(x) \le f(x) \text{ for all } x \in \mathbf{R}^n.\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } h(x) \le g(x) \text{ for all } x \in \mathbf{R}^n.\\
&\text{Goal: conclude that } g \text{ is the maximum convex monotone underestimator of } f.
\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } f:\mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is convex.}\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } f \text{ is rotation-invariant.}\\

&\text{Goal: prove that } f \text{ is concave on } \mathbf{R}_{++}^n.
\end{aligned}\]

\[\begin{aligned}
&\text{Definition: } f, g : \mathbf{R}^n \to \mathbf{R}.\\
&\text{Definition: } f(x) = \|x\|_1.\\
&\text{Definition: } g(x) = \frac{1}{2}\|x\|_2^2.\\
&\text{Definition: } h(x) = \inf_{y \in \mathbf{R}^n} \left( f(y) + g(x-y) \right).\\
&\text{Definition: } h(x) = \inf_{y \in \mathbf{R}^n} \left( \|y\|_1 + \frac{1}{2}\|x-y\|_2^2 \right).\\
&\text{Definition: } \phi : \mathbf{R} \to \mathbf{R}.\\
&\text{Definition: } \phi(u) = \begin{cases} u^2/2, & |u| \le 1,\\ |u| - 1/2, & |u| > 1. \end{cases}\\
&\text{Hypothesis: } x = (x_1, \dots, x_n) \in \mathbf{R}^n.\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } h(x) = \sum_{i=1}^n \phi(x_i).\\
&\text{Goal: prove that } h \text{ is the Huber penalty.}
\end{aligned}\]
